perturbation.original_text: A. “Events” shall mean all interoperability testing events, TestFests and Bi-Lateral Test Sessions, occurring after the date of signing this Agreement which are admi nistered by the Open Mobile Alliance and attended by Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: A. “Events” shall mean specific interoperability testing events, TestFests and Bi-Lateral Test Sessions, occurring after the date of signing this Agreement which are admi nistered by the Open Mobile Alliance and attended by Participants. These events are solely defined by OMA's written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Events' is broad. By adding 'specific' and 'solely defined by OMA's written notice,' we introduce ambiguity. Previously, any interoperability testing event could be considered an 'Event' under the agreement. Now, it's unclear whether an event qualifies if OMA hasn't explicitly designated it in writing. This contradicts section 3, which assumes all participants at all interoperability testing events administered by OMA are bound by this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. PURPOSE AND USE The purpose of this Agreement is to permit a Disclosing Participant to disclose and a Receiving Participant to receive informatio n relating to technologies of the Participants at the Events. All Participants at the Events must execute and abide by the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Participant agrees that Confidential Information may only be used for purposes of parti cipating in the Events, performing interoperability testing, generating test reports and modifying the Participant’s own technology or product (without restricting the Participant’s right to sell or market) to address identified interoperability problems. The Receiving Participant also agrees that the Confidential Information may only be disclosed subject to the terms of this Agreement internally to the Receiving Participant’s representatives who: (i) need to know the Confidential Information; (ii) understand that they are bound by this Agreement; and (iii) agree to fulfill the obligations of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Participant may use Residuals for any purpose, including use in the development, manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products and services . This right to use Residuals does not represent a license to any of Disclosing Participant ’s patents, copyrights, trademarks, and/or mask work rights. The term “Residuals” means information contained in the Confidential Information disclosed at the Events such as general knowledge, ideas, concepts, know -how, professional skills, work experience and/or techniques (not information such as exact implementations, Test Results, or Individual Results ) that is retained in the unaided memories of Receiving Participant ’s Representatives who have had access to Disclosing Participant ’s Confidential Information pursuant to the terms of this Agreement. A Representative’s memory is unaided if the Representat ive has not intentionally memorized, or referred to records of, such Confidential Information of Disclosing Participant, for the purpose of retaining and subsequently using or disclosing it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Events' to be 'solely defined by OMA's written notice.' This directly contradicts section 3, which states that 'All Participants at the Events must execute and abide by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.' The contradiction arises because under the new definition, it's unclear if an event is covered by the agreement if OMA hasn't explicitly designated it in writing, even if participants are present. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as it's no longer clear which events are covered and which participants are bound by the agreement, conflicting with the intention of section 3.

perturbation.original_text: C. “Disclosing Participant” shall be the Participant and its Representatives that disclose or otherwise make available the Confidential Information, as defined below. As used in this Agreement, the “Representatives” stated above shall mean su ch party’s officers, employees, agents, consultants or other representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: C. “Disclosing Participant” shall be the Participant that discloses or otherwise makes available the Confidential Information, as defined below. 'Representatives' are specifically excluded from being considered 'Disclosing Participants'.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and its Representatives' and explicitly excluding 'Representatives', we create a contradiction. Section 7 makes the Receiving Participant liable for unauthorized disclosure by persons or entities to whom the Receiving Participant has the right to disclose Confidential Information under this agreement. If Representatives are no longer Disclosing Participants, their disclosures may not trigger the same liability for the Receiving Participant, creating uncertainty about liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. LIABILITY AND LIMITATION OF LIABILITY The Receiving Participant shall be liable for: (i) any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Participant, and (ii) any unauthorized discl osure or use of Confidential Information by persons or entities to whom the Receiving Participant has the right to disclose Confidential Information under this Agreement, EXCEPT THAT, TO THE FULL EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, THE RECEIVING PARTICIPANT SHALL N OT BE LIABLE FOR, AND HEREBY DISCLAIMS ANY LIABILITY FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, PUNITIVE, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, LOSS OF BUSINESS, REVENUE, PROFITS, GOODWILL, USE, DATA, OR OTHER ECONOMIC ADV ANTAGE) ARISING OUT OF, OR IN CONNECTION WITH INADVERTENT OR SUCH UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE IF IT HAS EXERCISED THE SAME AMOUNT OF DILIGENCE IN PRESERVING THE SECRECY OF THAT INFORMATION AS THE DILIGENCE USED IN PRESERVING THE SECRECY OF THE RECEIVING PARTIC IPANT’S OWN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION OF LIKE IMPORTANCE, BUT IN NO EVENT LESS THAN REASONABLE DILIGENCE, AND, UPON BECOMING AWARE OF SUCH DISCLOSURE, NOTIFIED THE DISCLOSING PARTICIPANT THEREOF AND TAKEN REASONABLE MEASURES TO MITIGATE THE EFFECTS OF SUCH DISCLOSURE AND TO PREVENT ANY FURTHER DISCLOSURE. In the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Participant may take any and all actions under law against the Receiving Participant. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that the Open Mobil e Alliance is not responsible for any actions of its members, including, but not limited to, any breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Disclosing Participant' as the Participant *and* its Representatives. The changed text explicitly excludes 'Representatives' from being considered 'Disclosing Participants'. Section 7 makes the Receiving Participant liable for unauthorized disclosure by those to whom they have the right to disclose Confidential Information, but this now creates ambiguity as to whether the Receiving Participant is liable for unauthorized disclosures by the Disclosing Participant's Representatives, creating a contradiction. The explanation accurately describes how the change causes a contradiction to Section 7 by creating uncertainty about liability of 'Representatives' when there was not uncertainty before, and the change introduces uncertainty where the contradicted clause expects certainty, since the Receiving Participant may not be liable for breaches caused by 'Representatives' disclosures.

perturbation.original_text: E. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information provided at, pursuant to, for the purposes of, or dis covered or generated during (a) the Events and (b) the process set forth in the OMA Interoperability Policy and Process Document, including, but not limited to, the implementation conformance statements, test results, TestFest NDA 2017 Confidential Page 2 of 8 test reports, and the operator network configuration information, which: (i) is identified in written or oral format by the Disclosing Participant as confidential, trade secret or proprietary information at the time of disclosure, (provided that such information, if disclosed orally, must be summ arized and designated as confidential in written format to the Receiving Participant within thirty (30) days of disclosure), (ii) is identified in this Agreement as Confidential Information, or (iii) a reasonable person in the same circumstances as the Receiving Participant should have known or has reason to know is confidential, trade secret or proprietary information of the Disclosing Participant. Techniques and concepts which are commonly known in the industry shall not be considered to be Confidential Informatio n.
perturbation.changed_text: E. “Confidential Information” shall exclusively mean all information explicitly marked as such in a tangible form. Information disclosed orally shall not be considered Confidential Information under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes information disclosed orally, provided it is summarized and designated as confidential in writing within 30 days. The modified definition eliminates oral disclosures altogether. This creates a contradiction with Section 5, which states the Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information. If information is disclosed orally but not marked in writing, it is unclear whether the Receiving Participant has an obligation to keep it confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. DISCLOSURE The Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information, not disclose the Confidential Information except to Receiving Participant ’s Representatives in acc ordance with this Agreement, and will exercise the same amount of diligence in preserving the secrecy of that information as the diligence used in preserving the secrecy of the Receiving Participant’s Confidential Information of like importance, but in no event less than reasonable diligence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in definition E of "Confidential Information" now exclusively considers information explicitly marked in tangible form as confidential, and explicitly excludes orally disclosed information under any circumstances. This directly contradicts Section 5, which obligates the Receiving Participant to hold in confidence the Confidential Information. The original definition of Confidential Information includes information disclosed orally (if subsequently documented). This change creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the obligations related to orally disclosed information, leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: G. “TestFest ” shall mean an interoperability testing event open to all Open Mobile Alliance members and the public , organized by the Open Mobile Alliance staff, to permit the testing of one or more enablers.
perturbation.changed_text: G. “TestFest ” shall mean an interoperability testing event exclusively for Open Mobile Alliance members , organized by the Open Mobile Alliance staff, to permit the testing of one or more enablers.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition explicitly states that TestFests are open to the public. The modified definition restricts them to Open Mobile Alliance members only. This directly contradicts section 1, paragraph A, definition of Events and its inclusion of TestFests that are open to public.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. “Events” shall mean all interoperability testing events, TestFests and Bi-Lateral Test Sessions, occurring after the date of signing this Agreement which are admi nistered by the Open Mobile Alliance and attended by Participants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "TestFest" from being open to the public to being exclusively for Open Mobile Alliance members. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines "Events" to include "TestFests" that are, by implication from the original definition, open to the public. The change creates a clear and direct conflict by restricting access, thus breaking compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 2. EXISTING OBLIGATIONS The Participant acknowledges and agrees that its confidentiality obligations under the Application Form include any information whic h the Participant receives or which is discovered or generated during or as a result of its participation in the Events and which fall under the definition of Confidential Information. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that in the event of a conflic t between the terms of this Agreement and of the Application Form that the terms and obligations of the Application Form shall control, provided, however, in all situations the terms which provide the most extensive protection of the Participants’ results and information shall apply regardless of whether such reside in the Application Form or this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. EXISTING OBLIGATIONS The Participant acknowledges and agrees that its confidentiality obligations under the Application Form do not apply to this agreement in any sense, this agreement is exclusive.
perturbation.explanation: The original section specifies that confidentiality obligations under the Application Form apply and that the Application Form controls in case of conflict, provided it offers more extensive protection. The change makes the NDA agreement stand on its own regardless of the protection that the application form offers to any party. This is a contradiction in terms of the contractual obligations that are to be enforced between the application form and this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement. No modification to the Agreement will be binding, unless in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Application Form's confidentiality obligations apply and control in case of conflict, especially if they offer more protection. The changed text explicitly states that the Application Form's obligations do not apply. This directly contradicts section 15, which states that the Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement. The change removes the Application Form from consideration regarding confidentiality, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency. This aligns with the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: 11. TERM This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 or until supersede d by another non -disclosure agreement between the Open Mobile Alliance and Participant, provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall cease to apply to each item of Confidential Information five (5) years after such item of Confidential Information has been given, disclosed, required or supplied at or for the purposes of an Event.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. TERM This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 and the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement will be in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause provides for a sunset provision where confidentiality obligations cease 5 years after the disclosure of Confidential Information. The modified clause removes this sunset provision, making the confidentiality obligations perpetual. This introduces a contradiction because the original clause had a defined expiry date after which the data is no longer protected by the NDA while the new change the data and obligations have to be enforced forever.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. DISCLOSURE The Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information, not disclose the Confidential Information except to Receiving Participant ’s Representatives in acc ordance with this Agreement, and will exercise the same amount of diligence in preserving the secrecy of that information as the diligence used in preserving the secrecy of the Receiving Participant’s Confidential Information of like importance, but in no event less than reasonable diligence.
perturbation.justification: The original clause in Section 11 allowed for confidentiality obligations to cease after five years, while the modified clause removes this sunset provision, making the confidentiality obligations perpetual. This directly contradicts the general obligation in Section 5, which, while demanding a high level of diligence in preserving confidentiality, does not explicitly state it must be enforced indefinitely. The introduction of an indefinite obligation where a defined expiry previously existed creates a strong in-text contradiction.
